texto: If we want things to stay as they are, things will have to change.

texto: She was tall and well-made, on an ample scale; her skin looked as if it had the flavour of fresh cream which it resembled, her childlike mouth that of strawberries. Under a mass of raven hair, curling in gentle waves, her green eyes gleamed motionless as those of statues, and like them a little cruel. She was moving slowly, making her wide white skirt rotate around her, and emanating from her whole person the invincible calm of a woman sure of her own beauty.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “She was tall and well-made, on an ample scale; her skin looked as if it had the flavour of fresh …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, attractiveness, beauty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de beauty e confidence.

texto: They are coming to teach us good manners!" I replied in English. "But they won't succeed, because we are gods.

texto: Como siempre, la consideración de su muerte lo serenaba tanto como lo turbaba la muerte de los demás. Tal vez porque, a fin de cuentas, su muerte era el final del mundo.

texto: The truth is that he wanted to draw a little comfort from gazing at the stars. There were still one or two up there, at the zenith. As always, seeing them revived him; they were distant, they were omnipotent and at the same time they were docile to his calculations; just the contrary to humans, always too near, so weak and yet so quarrelsome.
explicacao: Citação de Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The truth is that he wanted to draw a little comfort from gazing at the stars. texto: There had been no enemies, just one single adversary, herself; her future had been killed by her own imprudence, by the reckless Salina pride; and now, just at the moment when her memories had come alive again after so many years, she found herself even without the solace of being able to blame her own unhappiness on others, a solace which is the last protective device of the desperate.
explicacao: Citação de Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “There had been no enemies, just one single adversary, herself; her future had been killed by her …” articula felicidade, desperation, enemies em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como enemies e unhappiness.

texto: A man of forty-five can consider himself still young till the moment comes when he realises that he has children old enough to fall in love.
explicacao: Citação de Giuseppe Tomasi di Lampedusa sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “A man of forty-five can consider himself still young till the moment comes when he realises that …” articula familia, falling-in-love, old-age em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como old-age e young.
